final_verdict: PASS (Round 5 nightmare review, 7 issues all resolved)
round5_key_changes: R5-01: Gauge dimension separated into d_g^block and d_g^suf = K*d_g^block + d (final norm <-> untied lm_head)
R5-02: Thm 5 W_{lm,ext} replaced by query-dependent L_i := dZ/dH_L (absorbs final RMSNorm Jacobian)
R5-03: Thm 4 proof rewritten with post-final-RMSNorm pre-logit state h̃_L[t_j]
R5-04: Thm 5 uniform contraction now over (i, j) both: sup_{i,j} σ_max(D_{i,j}) ≤ ρ < 1
R5-05: Removed false 'residual σ_max ≥ 1' claim; reframed as empirical question
R5-07: d_g ambiguity resolved via new Remark rem:gauge-dims; minor symbol overloads (T, K) remain cosmetic

thm:rank-bound.label: Gramian rank upper bound for suffix block

thm:screening.label: Depth screening
thm:screening.note: The subexponential-decay sub-case is stated qualitatively; the decomposition and uniform-contraction bound are proved formally.

residual_non_blocking: Attention gauge not implemented in code (disclosed; lies in ker(G) by Prop 1)
Subexponential case of thm:screening stated qualitatively (main paper uses the decomposition descriptively, not to certify any specific rate)
Cosine metric not gauge-invariant for Q/K/V/O in primary evaluation (disclosed; MLP 87% of params unaffected; gauge-invariant W_V shown separately in Table 9)
Cross-block symmetries not formally ruled out (paper no longer claims completeness)
Cosmetic T/K symbol overload in §3 (teacher vs sequence length; suffix depth vs observed positions)
Code expected_gauge_dimensions() uses old scalar attention count
